Article Title: Close shave: Chhagan Bhujbal’s helicopter lands in parking area in Pune- watch
Article Text:
.

NEW DELHI: Maharashtra cabinet minister and NCP leader Chhagan Bhujbal had a close call on Saturday when the helicopter he was travelling in landed in a parking lot in Pune instead of the designated helipad.Officials said a proper helipad had been prepared for VVIP arrivals, but the pilot reportedly mistook an open space in the parking area for the landing site.The area was relatively empty at the time, which may have led to the confusion. However, the landing was risky as the helicopter’s spinning rotors could have caused damage to nearby vehicles or structures.Speaking to reporters later, Bhujbal said, “There has been no accident; we have all emerged safely. There is no need to worry; the helicopter landed in the car parking area simply because the helipad was not visible.”Security agencies have started an inquiry to find out whether the mistake happened due to a technical problem or human error.Bhujbal was travelling from Nashik to Khanvadi in Purandar tehsil to attend an event marking the birth anniversary of Mahatma Jyotirao Phule.
Article Summary: Officials said a proper helipad had been prepared for VVIP arrivals. Pilot reportedly mistook an open space in the parking area for the landing site. Bhujbal was travelling from Nashik to Khanvadi in Purandar tehsil.

Article Title: Pakistan-linked handler targeting youth, two men arrested in Bijnor tell police
Article Text:
Representational photo

Watch Pakistan’s Terror Links Under Global Lens, US Congressional Report Echoes India’s Claim On Terrorism Pakistan’s Terror Links Under Global Lens, US Congressional Report Echoes India’s Claim On Terrorism

Poll Do you believe social media can be used to spread harmful ideologies? Yes, definitely No, not really

NEW DELHI: Two men arrested in Uttar Pradesh's Bijnor have told police that a Pakistan-linked handler was trying to build a network in India using social media platforms. The handler is currently based in Saudi Arabia and had been reaching out to people online, police said on Saturday.Uvaid Malik and Jalal Haider were arrested after a tip-off about their alleged links with Aqib, a native of Sathla village in Meerut’s Mawana area, who is now in West Asia. Aqib had earlier come under attention after a video surfaced on Instagram on November 23, where he was allegedly seen with an AK-47 rifle and explosives, circle officer Najibabad, Anjani Kumar Chaturvedi, said on Saturday.During questioning, the two accused told police that Aqib used Instagram to spread inflammatory content and incite youngsters against Hindus. Police said he also encouraged anti-national activities through his posts.Investigators found that Uvaid and Jalal had come in contact with Aqib and another person, Maizul, around three years ago while working in Surat. Maizul is currently based in South Africa and is believed to be part of the same network.Police said Uvaid was planning to flee to Nepal on the advice of his contacts. Teams are now searching for Aqib, Maizul, Sameer and Azad in connection with the case.
Article Summary: Two men arrested in Uttar Pradesh's Bijnor have told police that a Pakistan- linked handler was trying to build a network in India using social media platforms. The handler is currently based in Saudi Arabia and had been reaching out to people online, police said.

Article Title: 'Serious questions on Vishwaguru’s huglomacy': Congress slams PM Modi over US-Iran talks in Pakistan
Article Text:
Jairam Ramesh

Watch 'Chokepoints Now A Global Anxiety': Jaishankar Invokes West Asia Crisis At Indian Ocean Conference 'Chokepoints Now A Global Anxiety': Jaishankar Invokes West Asia Crisis At Indian Ocean Conference

The Congress leader also raised four key questions over the development:

How has Pakistan managed to carve out a new role for itself despite its role in the dastardly Pahalgam terror attack of April 2025 and the diplomatic engagement India mounted to isolate it following the attacks? This failure is especially damming because the Dr Manmohan Singh Government had very effectively isolated Pakistan after the Mumbai terror attacks in Nov 2008. How has India allowed the US to accord Pakistan this new role even after the Namaste Trump, Howdy Modi, and Phir Ek Baar Trump Sarkar campaigns of Mr. Modi and his cheerleaders? India also agreed to a very clearly one-sided trade deal where it gave much more than what it could get - and yet the Modi Government failed the gain any leverage with the US. Why didn’t India as the current President of BRICS+ launch any peace or mediation initiative - especially since Iran, the UAE, and Saudi Arabia are members of the BRICs+? What has India gained from its callibrated capitulation to China in the past eighteen months - especially in view of China’s pivotal role in Pakistan’s response to Operation Sindoor and its continued propping up of Pakistan?

Poll Should India take a more aggressive approach in its foreign policy towards Pakistan? Yes, definitely No, it should remain cautious

NEW DELHI: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh on Saturday jabbed Prime Minister Narendra Modi , questioning the government’s failure to prevent Washington from according Islamabad a key role in mediating between the United States and Iran.This comes after US Vice President JD Vance arrived in Islamabad to lead crucial talks with an Iranian delegation aimed at stabilising a fragile ceasefire and exploring a broader peace process in West Asia.Reacting to the development, Ramesh expressed hope for peace but raised concerns over India’s diplomatic position.In a post on X, he wrote: "The US-Iran meeting is beginning today in Islamabad. The entire world, including India, are hoping that this is the beginning of a durable peace process between the two countries that will not get derailed by Israel’s continuing agression in its neighbourhood."He then questioned the government’s foreign policy approach, referring to what he called the Prime Minister’s 'huglomacy'."But serious questions about the substance and style of the self-declared Vishwaguru’s huglomacy arise," he wrote."Peace in West Asia must return quickly. The Strait of Hormuz must once again revert to the situation that prevailed before the US-Israel assault on Iran began on Feb 28th - a mere two days after Mr.Modi had completed a most ill-advised and ill-timed visit to Israel," the Congress leader wrote.Ramesh’s remarks come amid heightened diplomatic tensions linked to the ongoing conflict, which began after US and Israeli strikes on Iran in late February.The war has disrupted global energy flows, particularly through the Strait of Hormuz, pushing up oil prices and increasing pressure for a negotiated settlement.The Congress leader also alleged that India failed to leverage its ties with Washington despite high-profile engagements such as 'Namaste Trump' and 'Howdy Modi'.The talks in Islamabad are taking place under tight security, with roads sealed and movement restricted in parts of the city. Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has described the negotiations as a “make-or-break” moment for regional stability.The Iranian delegation, led by Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, is expected to push for key conditions, including a ceasefire in Lebanon and the release of blocked Iranian assets.US Vice President JD Vance, ahead of the talks, warned Tehran against attempting to exploit the negotiations but also expressed cautious optimism about a positive outcome.The developments come against the backdrop of continued tensions in the region, particularly in Lebanon, and ongoing disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, which carries nearly 20 per cent of the world’s energy supply.Ramesh has been a vocal critic of the government’s handling of the conflict. In an earlier post, he had accused the Centre of remaining silent on the US-Israel strikes on Iran and failing to take any meaningful diplomatic initiative to de-escalate the situation.
Article Summary: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh questions India's failure to prevent Washington from according Islamabad a key role in mediating between the United States and Iran. He also alleged that India failed to leverage its ties with Washington despite high-profile engagements such as 'Namaste Trump' and 'Howdy Modi'

Article Title: 'Unlike acting, no retakes in governance': P Chidambaram 'cautions' Vijay ahead of Tamil Nadu polls
Article Text:
Vijay; P Chidambaram

Watch How Stalin, EPS, Vijay Are Outbidding Each Other On Freebies & 1 Party Is Opposing It | I Witness How Stalin, EPS, Vijay Are Outbidding Each Other On Freebies & 1 Party Is Opposing It | I Witness

NEW DELHI: Senior Congress leader P Chidambaram on Saturday took a swipe at Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) chief Vijay , remarking that "governance offers no retakes" — in a pointed reference to the actor-politician’s film career.“Governance is not easy. I have governed for about 20 years, and I can say it is not a simple task. In governance, there are no retakes,” Chidambaram said during a roadshow in Sivaganga. He was referring to his decades-long political career, during which he held key portfolios such as home and finance, among others, in various Congress-led governments at the Centre.“In cinema, if a scene doesn’t come out well, the director can ask for another take. But in administration, that’s not possible. Once a decision is made, there is no retake. The decision stands as it is,” the senior Congressman added.Vijay, a superstar in Tamil cinema, launched TVK in February 2024. The party is contesting all 234 constituencies in Tamil Nadu, where assembly elections will be held on April 23, with counting scheduled for May 4.Meanwhile, Chidambaram also criticised Vijay for skipping his scheduled speech at Friday’s election rally in Karaikudi.“He left without even speaking. How is that acceptable? Politics is about speaking, isn’t it? He has not met the press. He is not interacting with the people either. Look at me, I come to you. I have served as home minister and finance minister. I travel alone by car. Every time I come, I am ready to speak. I am always prepared for a debate if there is one,” the Rajya Sabha MP stated.According to TVK sources, Vijay could not address the rally due to a delay in reaching Karaikudi from Madurai, a distance of around 100 km. They attributed the delay to "massive crowds" along the route, pushing his arrival at the venue back by nearly four hours.By the time his convoy reached the venue, the official time permitted by the police for the event had expired, forcing the TVK chief to briefly wave to the crowd before leaving.
Article Summary: Senior Congress leader P Chidambaram took a swipe at Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) chief Vijay. He said "governance offers no retakes" — in a pointed reference to the actor-politician’s film career. Vijay, a superstar in Tamil cinema, launched TVK in February 2024.

Article Title: TMC, BJP supporters clash during campaign in West Bengal’s Hooghly; FIR lodged
Article Text:
BJP leader Biman Ghosh (Photo credit: ANI)

Watch Suvendu Adhikari, Samik Bhattacharya Or Dilip Ghosh? What Amit Shah Said on BJP's Bengal CM Face Suvendu Adhikari, Samik Bhattacharya Or Dilip Ghosh? What Amit Shah Said on BJP's Bengal CM Face

NEW DELHI: Supporters of the Trinamool Congress and the Bharatiya Janata Party clashed during an election campaign on Friday in the Hooghly district of West Bengal, news agency ANI reported.Biman Ghosh, BJP's candidate from the Pursurah Assembly constituency, alleged that four party workers sustained minor injuries in the incident. He also said that a FIR is being filed at the local police station.Speaking to ANI, Ghosh accused TMC workers of disrupting the campaign and warned of a strong electoral outcome for the BJP. He said, "They (the TMC members) were creating a ruckus during the election campaign; they, too, must realise what awaits them after the 4th, when a BJP 'tsunami' sweeps across Bengal. It would be best for them to mend their ways before it is too late. Four of our workers have sustained minor injuries, and we are filing an FIR at the police station".The incident comes at a time when campaigning has intensified ahead of the 2026 West Bengal Assembly elections, with both parties trading sharp political attacks.Earlier on Friday, the ruling Trinamool Congress and the BJP engaged in a war of words over the BJP’s recently released Bengal Sankalp Patra. The document, themed around “Sonar Bangla” (Golden Bengal), outlines a proposed shift in governance, with a focus on national security, youth employment and women’s safety.Launching the manifesto in Kolkata, Union home minister Amit Shah described it as a roadmap to transform governance in the state and align it with the broader vision of a “Viksit Bharat”.Shah also criticised the Trinamool Congress government, alleging that 15 years of its rule had resulted in a breakdown of law and order and widespread public dissatisfaction. He said the manifesto aimed to bring “every section of Bengal out of despair” and offer “a new way forward for farmers surrounded by fear”.He further claimed that people in the state were “frightened and disillusioned” and were seeking change, reiterating that the BJP’s governance model aligns with the vision of national development.Polling for the 294-member West Bengal Assembly will be conducted in two phases on April 23 and April 29, with counting scheduled for May 4.
Article Summary: Supporters of the Trinamool Congress and the Bharatiya Janata Party clashed during an election campaign on Friday. Biman Ghosh, BJP's candidate from the Pursurah Assembly constituency, alleged that four party workers sustained minor injuries in the incident.

Article Title: Sabarimala Temple: 'Not good for Hinduism': Supreme Court flags risks of sect-based temple restrictions during Sabarimala hearing
Article Text:
(Image credits: PTI)

Watch Kerala LDF Government Backs Ban on Women at Sabarimala Temple: Political and Electoral Context Kerala LDF Government Backs Ban on Women at Sabarimala Temple: Political and Electoral Context

NEW DELHI: The Supreme Court on Thursday expressed concern over arguments supporting sect-based restrictions in temples and mutts, saying such exclusions could adversely affect Hinduism and divide society.The remarks came from a nine-judge Constitution bench headed by Chief Justice Surya Kant while hearing a batch of petitions concerning discrimination against women at religious places, including the Sabarimala temple , and the extent of religious freedom available to different faiths and denominations.The bench also comprised Justices B V Nagarathna, M M Sundresh, Ahsanuddin Amanullah, Aravind Kumar, Augustine George Masih, Prasanna B Varale, R Mahadevan, and Joymalya Bagchi.Senior advocate C S Vaidyanathan, appearing for devotees of Lord Ayyappa of the Sabarimala temple in Kerala, argued that Article 26(b) of the Constitution gives a religious denomination the right to manage its own affairs and would prevail over Article 25(2)(b), which empowers the State to throw open Hindu religious institutions of a public nature to all sections of society.Appearing for the Nair Service Society and other organisations linked to Lord Ayyappa devotees, Vaidyanathan argued that they constitute a separate religious denomination and therefore have the right to manage the affairs of the hilltop temple.He submitted that a denominational temple can permit worship or confine it only to members of that denomination, while maintaining that public temples must remain open to all.On the issue of access to the Sabarimala temple, he said, "In Sabarimala, there is no distinction made between devotees. There is no bar to Christians or Muslims even, but they must have faith and belief in the divinity of Lord Ayyappa and they have to follow rituals like the 40-day Vratam and whatever practices enjoined on the believers. Nobody is prohibited and therefore, this concept has not been understood."Vaidyanathan also maintained that giving primacy to Article 25(2)(b) over Article 26(b) would uniquely affect Hinduism, since Article 25(2)(b) specifically enables the State to make laws opening Hindu temples to all sections of society.He also told the court that there are private family temples in Kerala where only members of particular families worship and such temples serve only their denomination. Such temples, he said, cannot seek funds from the State, private donors or the public because they are not dependent on them.He argued that if a law is to be made, it must pass the test of public order, morality or health.He also submitted that an individual’s freedom of conscience defeats the freedom of the community or denomination.Referring to the 2018 Sabarimala verdict, Vaidyanathan said Justice (retired) D Y Chandrachud had framed the issue incorrectly."He asked whether individual rights would prevail over group or collective rights and he held wrongly that individual rights can prevail over collective religious rights," he submitted.This line of argument drew concern from the bench, particularly over its wider impact on Hindu religious practice.Justice B V Nagarathna said, "There is one apprehension. If you say the right of entry, in the context of (the) Venkataramana Devaru (judgment of the apex court), where they said anybody other than Gowda Saraswat Brahmins is excluded, it will negatively affect Hinduism."She added, "Everybody must have access to every temple and mutt. Keep aside the controversy in the Sabarimala judgment (2018). But if you say it is a practice and it is a matter of religion that I will exclude others and only my section, my denomination, will attend the temple and nobody else, that is not good for Hinduism. Let the religion not be adversely affected. It will be counter-productive for the denomination."Justice Aravind Kumar agreed, saying such exclusions would divide society.Justice Nagarathna further said, "If the argument is that only Gowda Saraswat Brahmins must come to a temple, the followers of the Kanchi Mutt must only go to Kanchi, they should not go to Sringeri, the followers of Sringeri must not go to Kanchi, then it will affect the religion".She also said the State can step in under Article 25(2)(b) to ensure access to temples for all sections of society.Justice Kumar, responding to Vaidyanathan’s submission that Article 26(b) supersedes Article 25(2)(b), said, "That is why we said, do not pitch the argument too high,"Justice Nagarathna clarified that she was not referring to private family temples and said, "Let the religion not be adversely affected."During the hearing, the bench also discussed the Devaru judgment of 1957, in which the Supreme Court upheld the Madras Temple Entry Authorisation Act. The ruling held that while a temple remains open to all Hindus, certain ceremonial practices reserved for the Gowda Saraswath Brahmins are constitutionally permissible.Justice Kumar also asked Vaidyanathan whether he agreed that Article 26 is not a standalone provision and whether it must be read along with Article 25(2), in line with the Union government’s position.Vaidyanathan said he disagreed with the Centre’s stand, arguing that Article 25(2) is an enabling provision and does not confer any right, while Article 26 grants a specific right to a denomination.The hearing will resume next week.
Article Summary: Supreme Court expresses concern over arguments supporting sect-based restrictions in temples and mutts. Justices say such exclusions could adversely affect Hinduism and divide society. Remarks came from a nine-judge Constitution bench headed by Chief Justice Surya Kant.

Article Title: ‘Victims allegedly forced to offer namaz, eat beef, sexually harassed’: What we know about Nashik IT firm case
Article Text:
A special investigation team (SIT) is probing a series of sexual harassment cases at a multinational IT company in Nashik

Arrests and court remand

Multiple FIRs and allegations

PoSH Act compliance under scanner

Poll What do you think is the most pressing issue highlighted by this case? Sexual Harassment Negligence of HR Religious Sentiment Hurting

Chief minister’s response

NASHIK: A special investigation team (SIT) is probing a series of sexual harassment cases at a multinational IT company in Nashik, after eight women employees lodged complaints alleging mental and sexual harassment over the past few years.Seven people, including an assistant general manager (HR), have been arrested in the case.Speaking to reporters, Maharashtra minister Girish Mahajan said the probe could uncover a wider pattern.“Some senior employees, including women, did not take any action despite complaints. A case has been filed against them. The inquiry is underway. I think the involvement of some more people will also come to light in this,” he said.The minister alleged that the victims had been forced to offer namaz, eat beef, and that attempts had been made to convert them."Some senior employees, including women, did not take any action despite complaints. A case has been filed against them. The inquiry is underway. I think the involvement of some more people will also come to light in this," he said.He further claimed that the women might have been victims of a racket involved in luring girls with a promise of jobs and later blackmailing them with their photographs and videos.He further said the investigation was not limited to one workplace.“Authorities suspect this may not be an isolated incident and could reflect a larger trend within major corporate environments,” he said.Police said the HR official was arrested for allegedly abetting sexual harassment by failing to act on a complaint raised by a woman employee against colleagues.The court remanded the HR manager to police custody till April 13, while six other accused were sent to 14-day judicial custody.Assistant commissioner of Police (Crime) Sandeep Mitke, who is heading the SIT, said more arrests and custodial actions are likely.“We will seek the court’s production warrant to secure custody of these five accused and arrest them in the other cases,” he said. “We are also searching for one more suspect, whose whereabouts are not known yet,” he added.Police have registered nine FIRs since March 26, including cases of sexual harassment, breach of promise to marry, and allegations of hurting religious sentiments. The incidents are said to have taken place between 2022 and 2026.In one case, investigators allege that the HR official ignored a complaint of sexual harassment, allowing the accused employees to continue their actions.“Had this HR official taken note of the complaint, the other crimes would not have taken place. The ignorance of the HR official only encouraged the accused to indulge in sexual harassment of the victims,” assistant public prosecutor Aniket Avhad said.However, defence lawyer Baba Sayyad argued that further custodial remand was not required, stating that police had not recovered any material evidence from the accused.District collector Ayush Prasad has ordered a probe into compliance with the Prevention of Sexual Harassment (PoSH) Act at the company.“The inquiry by the police and the district-level committee will determine if the IT company has been taking necessary steps, including the proper registration of complaints and maintaining proactive vigilance as mandated by the PoSH Act,” he said.Officials said the district-level committee will examine whether the Internal Committee mandated under the PoSH Act existed and whether it functioned properly.ACP Mitke said another woman employee, named as a co-accused in the earlier case involving sexual assault and hurting religious sentiments, has been absconding since the complaint was filed on March 25.He added that the SIT is actively trying to track her down using technical surveillance and intelligence inputs, and expressed confidence that her arrest is imminent.Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis praised the police action in the case.“The way sexual exploitation was carried out and religious sentiments were hurt at an IT firm using pressure is shocking. I would like to congratulate the police for acting swiftly and exposing the matter,” he said.Police said the SIT continues to investigate all nine cases registered against employees of the firm, which employs around 300 people in Nashik.
Article Summary: A special investigation team (SIT) is probing a series of sexual harassment cases at a multinational IT company in Nashik. Eight women employees lodged complaints alleging mental and sexual harassment over the past few years. Seven people, including an assistant general manager (HR), have been arrested in the case.

Article Title: ‘Threw alcohol bottle after crash’: 21-year-old engineering student arrested for ramming SUV into scooter in Gurgaon; 2 dead
Article Text:
The accused, Akrit Yadav, a resident of Ghaziabad and an engineering student, was arrested by Sector 56 police following an FIR registered in connection with the April 4 crash.

Poll Do you think that young drivers are more prone to reckless behavior? Yes, definitely No, not really

GURGAON: Police have arrested a 21-year-old man for allegedly driving an SUV on the wrong side and crashing into a scooter in Sector 57 on April 4, killing two young men.The accused, Akrit Yadav, a resident of Ghaziabad and an engineering student, was arrested by Sector 56 police following an FIR registered in connection with the April 4 crash.The damaged Tata Harrier and the scooter involved in the accident were seized from the spot, and further investigation is underway, police said.The victims were identified as Kartik Suresh (24), an engineering student at a private university in the city, and Ritik Kumar (27), a native of Motihari in Bihar’s East Champaran district, who worked at a call centre in Sector 24 and also drove a bike taxi.According to police, the accident took place in front of Legend Society when the scooter, heading towards Golf Course Extension Road via Dharmamarg, was hit head-on by the SUV coming from the opposite direction. The impact threw both riders onto the road, leaving them with severe head injuries.Ritik was taken to a private hospital, while Kartik was shifted to a government facility by police. Both succumbed to their injuries during treatment.Kartik’s mother, Rajni, questioned the circumstances surrounding the crash and demanded a thorough probe. “The reckless driver, who himself is pursuing engineering, claimed two innocent lives and ruined two families forever.It is extremely painful to see that he got bail easily even after killing two people. I have lost my child and want justice. Police should check if the driver was under the influence or at least examine CCTV footage to ascertain the truth. We are yet to be provided any footage,” she said.An eyewitness who saw the aftermath from her balcony, alleged suspicious behaviour by the driver. “We saw the young man throw a bottle of alcohol out of the car minutes after the crash. There was also a cup inside. He was frantically making calls, got into the driver’s seat, and tried to drive away while calling someone to come,” she told TOI.She added that one of the victims appeared to have died on the spot, while the other was still breathing when bystanders rushed to help. “A cab driver stopped and asked us to hurry and put the injured man in his car. Both boys near the vehicle looked very young. The airbags had deployed and the passenger seat belt was loose,” she said.She also claimed that police reached the spot around 12 minutes after she alerted them.Police said the accused driver was arrested and further action in the case is being taken as per procedure. An FIR was registered at Sector 56 police station under sections 281 (rash driving), 106 (causing death by negligence), 324(4) (mischief), and 125(b) (act endangering life or personal safety of others) of the BNS.
Article Summary: Akrit Yadav, a resident of Ghaziabad and an engineering student, was arrested by Sector 56 police following an FIR registered in connection with the April 4 crash. The victims were identified as Kartik Suresh (24) and Ritik Kumar (27), a native of Motihari in Bihar’s East Champaran district. Both succumbed to their injuries during treatment.

Article Title: Andra Pradesh Girl Murder: ‘Entered house, slit her throat’: 19-year-old spurned man who 'killed' Andhra Pradesh girl tries to flee, shot in leg
Article Text:
Accused, cops shifted to RIMS

Poll What is your opinion on the reaction of community to the accused's relocation? Justified, they deserve justice Excessive, violence is not the solution

TIRUPATI: Police opened fire when the 19-year-old accused in the murder of a minor girl at Khazipet allegedly tried to flee while he was being shifted on Saturday morning.Khazipet police, who arrested A Venkatesh - who entered the girl's house on Friday when she was alone and slit her throat - kept him in the lock up at Mydukur police station overnight.Police said he was placed under custody at Mydukur police station and not at Khazipet owing to protests by the family of the victim and locals, demanding that he be handed over to them.As there was a possibility of an attack by relatives and locals on Mydukur police station as well, police decided to shift Venkatesh to another police station on Saturday morning.According to Kadapa SP Shelke Nachiket Vishwanath, the accused escaped when he was being shifted. When Khazipet police, led by CI S Vamsidhar, zeroed in on Venkatesh near Basapuram checkpost, he attacked them with a rod and injured two constables, Narasumhulu and Sivaiah.Khazipet police inspector warned the accused to surrender but he continued to display aggressive behaviour. Vamsidhar fired a warning shot in the air, but even then there was no change in Venkatesh's behaviour and he continued to advance towards the police.Left with no option, Vamsidhar fired two rounds on Venkatesh below his knees and brought the situation under control," Kadapa SP Shelke Nachiket Vishwanath said. After being administered first aid at govt hospital at Mydukur, the injured constables and Venkatesh were shifted amidst tight security to RIMS at Kadapa for treatment. "We promised the girl's family to render justice and will ensure that justice is delivered," he said
Article Summary: Police opened fire when the 19-year-old accused in the murder of a minor girl at Khazipet allegedly tried to flee. A Venkatesh entered the girl's house on Friday when she was alone and slit her throat. Police said he was placed under custody at Mydukur police station.

Article Title: 'Intended to marry minor girl for male child': Telangana businessman, 9 others held for murder of pregnant wife, 2 daughters
Article Text:
Bizman, 9 others held for murder of pregnant wife, minor daughters



Get real-time updates and result insights on

Get real-time updates and result insights on Assam HSLC 10th result

WARANGAL: About ten days after a three-month-pregnant woman and her two daughters, aged nine and five, were found dead in a private swimming pool on the outskirts of Warangal, police on Friday arrested ten individuals, including the woman's husband — the prime accused.According to Warangal police commissioner Sunpreeth Singh, Md Azharuddin allegedly killed his wife, Md Farhana (26), their two children, and her unborn foetus after learning that the foetus was female. Police said he intended to marry a minor girl in the hope of fathering a male child.The other arrested include Azharuddin's parents, Md Chand Pasha and Saleema, and his brother, Sharfuddin, who allegedly assisted in the crime. The others include Satla Raju, a medical shop owner, L Ramesh, an RMP practitioner, Dr Balne Parthu of Nekkonda mandal, Manohar, a lab technician from Shayampet, B Sravanthi, a nurse in Kazipet, and V Bhadru, a mediator. They are accused of facilitating an illegal prenatal sex determination test on Farhana.Police said two gynaecologists, B Purnima and S Ravali, allegedly involved in the case, are absconding.The investigation revealed that the sequence of events began in March, when Azharuddin allegedly forced his wife to undergo a sex determination test.He initially approached Satla Raju, who connected him with an RMP named Naresh, a resident of Kondaparthy in Kazipet mandal, along with lab technician Manohar. The group reportedly took the couple to Suraksha Hospital in Kazipet, where Dr S Ravali and nurse Sravanthi conducted the test. Upon learning that the foetus was female, Azharuddin is said to have conspired with his parents to carry out the murders.According to police, on April 1, Azharuddin took his family to his farmhouse near Punnelu village on the outskirts of Warangal, under the pretext of celebrating his younger daughter's birthday. The family resided in the village, where he operated a cement and brick manufacturing unit.At the farmhouse, he allegedly disconnected CCTV cameras and cut off the power supply before leading them to the pool and pushing them in. Police said he remained there until they drowned, then raised an alarm and attempted to pass off the deaths as accidental. He also informed Farhana's parents.However, Farhana's father, S K Ali, grew suspicious on seeing the bodies in the pool and lodged a complaint with the police.Following the complaint, special teams were formed and Azharuddin was arrested on the outskirts of the village. Police said he confessed to the crime during interrogation.He has been booked under Sections 103 (murder), 106(1) (causing death by negligence), and 238 (causing disappearance of evidence) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita. The other accused have been charged under Section 23 of the PCPNDT Act for sex selection and prenatal sex determination.Azharuddin has also been booked under Sections 11 and 12 of the POCSO Act for allegedly attempting to marry a minor, based on a complaint by her father, who said the accused continued to pursue her despite repeated rejection.
Article Summary: Md Azharuddin allegedly killed his wife, Md Farhana (26), their two children, and her unborn foetus after learning that the foetus was female. Police said he intended to marry a minor girl in the hope of fathering a male child.

Article Title: Hyderabad advocate killed as tanker overturns on BMW
Article Text:
Hyd advocate killed as tanker overturns on car in Andhra

Poll Do you think stricter regulations are needed for heavy vehicles on highways? Yes, definitely No, current regulations are sufficient

VIJAYAWADA: A fatal road accident at Keesara toll plaza in Kanchikacherla mandal of NTR district claimed the life of B Srinivasa Rao, a senior advocate from Hyderabad on Saturday after a fly ash tanker lost control and overturned onto his car.According to police, the accident occurred when the brakes of a 16-wheeled tanker reportedly failed while heading towards Hyderabad.The driver lost control near the toll plaza, causing the heavy vehicle to topple directly onto a BMW car passing through the stretch. The impact was so severe that the BMW was flattened under the weight of the tanker.Srinivasa was a resident of Yellareddyguda in Hyderabad. He was travelling alone in the vehicle at the time of the accident.Officials said the body was mangled due to the intensity of the crash.Rescue teams faced significant challenges in retrieving the body, as the tanker had to be lifted using cranes and earth-moving machines.The operation took considerable time before the wreckage could be cleared.Vijayawada police commissioner S V Rajasekhara Babu rushed to the spot and supervised the rescue efforts. He confirmed that only one person was in the car and that the accident was caused by loss of control of the tanker.State Bar Council president Vasireddy Prabhunath visited the accident site and expressed deep grief over the advocate's death, describing it as a tragic loss.Colleagues remembered Srinivasa Rao as a lawyer who rose from student activism and was known for his commitment to public issues and justice. Police have registered a case and are investigating the circumstances leading to the accident.
Article Summary: B Srinivasa Rao was a senior advocate from Hyderabad. He was travelling alone in the vehicle at the time of the accident. The impact was so severe that the BMW was flattened under the weight of the tanker. State Bar Council president Vasireddy Prabhunath expressed deep grief over the advocate's death.

Article Title: Madhya Pradesh Murder Case: 'Tum kaale ho, mujhe deserve nahi karte': Wife plots husband murder with lover; chilling details emerge
Article Text:
Priyanka and Devkrishna

A strained marriage

A horror in Dhar village

Police investigation and breakthrough

Poll Do you think media coverage impacts public perception in crime cases? Yes, significantly Not so much

Confession and arrests

INDORE: In a case that has all the elements of a dark crime drama, the killing of a man in Madhya Pradesh’s Dhar district has unravelled into a story of alleged betrayal, humiliation and a planned conspiracy.Family members revealed that Devkrishna's marriage had long been troubled.His sister Jyoti alleged that Priyanka, 25, would often humiliate him, saying, "Tum kaale ho, mujhe deserve nahi karte (You are dark-skinned, you don't deserve me.)"Also read: Woh aaye, maarke chale gaye’: Woman feigns grief, cooks up robbery story after plotting husband's murder Jyoti further claimed that tensions between the couple were frequent and escalating over time. She alleged that Priyanka would often attend weddings and social gatherings where her alleged lover was present, which led to repeated arguments at home.She also claimed she had suspected Priyanka’s affair since 2020. According to her, the couple’s relationship had been marked by regular fights and emotional strain. “Bhabhi used to stand at the window, and he used to pass on the road,” Jyoti alleged, describing moments she said reflected the growing distance between them.A late-night incident in Gondikheda Charan village initially appeared to be a robbery gone wrong, but later turned into a suspected case of planned murder involving the victim’s wife.Police said Devkrishna was found dead after what was first reported as a violent break-in at the house.The accused, Priyanka Purohit (27), told police that three to four unidentified men had entered the house at night, held the couple hostage in separate rooms, and fled with jewellery worth around Rs 3 lakh and Rs 50,000 in cash after killing her husband.However, investigators soon found inconsistencies in her statement.Jewellery allegedly stolen during the incident was recovered from inside the house, raising doubts about her account.Police also noted that the injuries on the victim suggested a targeted attack rather than a random burglary.Assistant superintendent of police Parul Belapurkar said missing mobile data, contradictions in Priyanka’s statements, and forensic findings pointed towards foul play.Within hours of sustained questioning, the narrative of a robbery began to collapse. Police said evidence suggested the attack had been planned in advance and staged to look like a burglary.Investigators also found that Kamlesh, Priyanka’s alleged partner, had reportedly paid around Rs 1 lakh to a man named Surendra to carry out the killing.Surendra is currently absconding.Police said Priyanka eventually confessed to plotting the murder along with Kamlesh, allegedly motivated by their relationship and a plan to remove her husband from the picture.Officials added that Priyanka had been married to Devkrishna since she was around 15 years old and had recently returned to her parental home shortly before the incident, during which the final plan is believed to have been executed.Both Priyanka and Kamlesh have been arrested, and a case has been registered under relevant provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS). Investigations are continuing.
Article Summary: Priyanka Purohit, 27, is accused of plotting the murder of her husband, Devkrishna. The couple's marriage had been marked by regular fights and emotional strain. Police said evidence suggested the attack had been planned in advance and staged to look like a burglary.

Article Title: Macabre twist in Ahmedabad ‘dosa’ deaths: Couple may have killed daughters over desire for son
Article Text:
Poll Do you think that the mental health of parents should be evaluated in such cases? Yes, it's crucial No, it’s not relevant



Get real-time updates and result insights on

Get real-time updates and result insights on Assam HSLC 10th result

AHMEDABAD: The investigation into the deaths of Raha and Mishri Prajapati — three months and four years old respectively — which was first ascribed to contaminated dosa batter, is now focusing on the possible murder of the girls because their parents wanted a son.A police officer said, "We found a diary from the house of the mother's parents." He said, "She wrote in it that she wanted a boy and vowed to visit a temple if her wish was fulfilled."Additional commissioner of police, Sector 1, Neeraj Badgujar, said that the father of the dead girls, Vimal Prajapati, was questioned.The girls' mother, Bhavna, is still hospitalized. Another officer said toxicology tests found aluminium phosphide, used in insecticides and rodenticides, in the parents' blood."We questioned Vimal and found that he bought 10 packets of a wheat preservative that contains aluminium phosphide. He used eight but could not say where the other two packets went," the officer said.The officer said Vimal's parents, Gaurishankar and Kusum Prajapati, were also questioned after police learned the family had constant quarrels over money.
Article Summary: The investigation into the deaths of Raha and Mishri Prajapati is now focusing on the possible murder of the girls because their parents wanted a son. A police officer said, "We found a diary from the house of the mother's parents" The girls' mother, Bhavna, is still hospitalized.

Article Title: Maharashtra Earthquake: 4.5 magnitude earthquake jolts Maharashtra's Hingoli; no casualties
Article Text:
Maharashtra’s Hingoli hit by 4.5-magnitude quake (Image used for representational purpose only)

CHHATRAPATI SAMBHAJINAGAR: An earthquake of magnitude 4.7 struck parts of Hingoli district in Maharashtra ’s Marathwada region on Saturday morning, with tremors also felt in neighbouring Nanded and Parbhani districts, officials said.There were no immediate reports of loss of life, they added.However, some houses and community halls in Pangra Shinde village developed cracks, Hingoli collector Rahul Gupta said over the phone.
Article Summary: Earthquake of magnitude 4.7 struck parts of Hingoli district in Maharashtra ’s Marathwada region. There were no immediate reports of loss of life. Some houses and community halls in Pangra Shinde village developed cracks.

Article Title: Newlywed Couple Killed In Accident: Caught on cam: Day after wedding, couple among 4 killed as truck overturns onto auto-rickshaw
Article Text: SHIVPURI: At least four people, including a newlywed bride and groom, were killed in a tragic road accident in Madhya Pradesh’s Shivpuri district after an out-of-control truck overturned onto a roadside auto.The incident occurred near a petrol pump on Tongra Road in the Sirsod police station area on Saturday afternoon around 12 pm.According to police, the truck, loaded with poultry feed, lost control and toppled onto an auto that was parked along the roadside.The victims, identified as the bride and groom, the groom’s mother and his sister-in-law, were seated inside the auto when the truck overturned onto them, crushing them to death on the spot.Police said the family was travelling to Rajgarh village after the wedding.The auto had briefly halted near the petrol pump when the accident took place.After nearly half an hour, the victims were extricated and taken to the district hospital, where doctors declared them dead.Police have registered a case and initiated an investigation into the incident.
Article Summary: The incident occurred near a petrol pump on Tongra Road in the Sirsod police station area. The victims, identified as the bride and groom, the groom’s mother and his sister-in-law, were seated inside the auto when the truck overturned onto them.

Article Title: Another woman, her lover kill husband; tried to pass it off as robbery
Article Text:
Another MP woman, her lover kill husband; tried to pass it off as robbery

Poll How do you feel about the use of psychological methods in police investigations? Very effective Somewhat effective

BHIND: A 23-year-old woman in Bhind has been accused of conspiring with her lover to kill her husband, in a case police say was initially staged as a robbery, news agency PTI reported.The incident comes days after a similar crime surfaced in Dhar.Police said the accused, identified as Ruby, and her lover Vishal Vimal (21) are in custody.A third suspect, Rajesh Kushwah, who allegedly assisted in the killing, remains absconding.According to superintendent of police Asit Yadav, the victim, Nilesh Jatav (27), was shot dead on April 8 while riding a motorcycle with his wife and their child.Police were initially told that unidentified robbers had carried out the attack. Ruby claimed that while they were travelling from Jamna to Kharika, her bag fell on the road.She said that when her husband stopped the motorcycle and she went to retrieve it, two men arrived, shot him and fled.A murder case was registered against unknown persons, and an investigation was launched.“During the investigation, the wife’s statements appeared suspicious. Police then questioned her using psychological methods, which led to the case being solved,” the SP said.Police later alleged that the killing had been planned in advance. Ruby was said to have been in a relationship with Vimal, a relative, for nearly seven years, and the two were in regular contact via WhatsApp.Investigators believe the woman saw her husband as an obstacle to the relationship.According to police, she deliberately dropped her bag at a pre-decided location to make her husband stop the motorcycle. At that point, Vimal and another associate allegedly shot him.Officers said they have seized a 12-bore country-made pistol, empty cartridges, a motorcycle without a registration number and mobile phones from the accused.Efforts are under way to locate the absconding suspect.In a similar case reported recently in Dhar district, a woman allegedly plotted her husband’s murder with her lover and hired a contract killer. Police said she too initially attempted to present the killing as a robbery before later confessing during questioning.
Article Summary: A 23-year-old woman in Bhind has been accused of conspiring with her lover to kill her husband. Nilesh Jatav (27) was shot dead on April 8 while riding a motorcycle with his wife and their child. Police said the accused, identified as Ruby, and her lover Vishal Vimal (21) are in custody.

Article Title: Mother Kills Children: Mother kills two minor children in Odisha, attempts suicide
Article Text: BHUBANESWAR: A woman allegedly killed her two minor children in Odisha’s Jharsuguda town in an incident police suspect was linked to a domestic dispute.The incident occurred on Saturday night.The accused, identified as Ranjita Oram, is alleged to have murdered her five-year-old son and 10-year-old daughter at their residence.Following the incident, she reportedly attempted to take her own life using a conventional vegetable cutter, police said.Both children were found dead at the scene.Police reached the spot and took the woman into custody.Preliminary investigation suggests that a domestic dispute may have triggered the incident, though the exact motive is yet to be ascertained.A case has been registered, and further investigation is underway.
Article Summary: The incident occurred on Saturday night in Odisha's Jharsuguda town. The accused, identified as Ranjita Oram, is alleged to have murdered her two minor children. She reportedly attempted to take her own life using a conventional vegetable cutter.

Article Title: Woman dies after jilted lover throws acid on her
Article Text: LUCKNOW: A 23-year-old woman died after a jilted lover allegedly threw acid on her and her mother while they were asleep inside their home in Morwan village under Ramkola police station in Kushinagar on Thursday night. The victim’s 58-year-old mother is battling for life.The deceased was Kajal Yadav , daughter of Prabhunath Yadav. Her mother, Leelawati Devi, sustained severe burn injuries and is undergoing treatment in critical condition at Gorakhpur Medical College.Police said the attack took place around 3:30am when the accused allegedly climbed onto the roof of the house and entered through a sliding window. The main gate of the house was locked from inside, indicating a planned intrusion. He then poured acid on the sleeping mother and daughter and fled the scene.Hearing their screams, family members rushed to the room and found both women severely burnt. They were taken to Ramkola community health centre and later referred to Kushinagar Medical College. Due to the critical nature of their injuries, both were shifted to Gorakhpur, where Kajal succumbed during treatment, while her mother continues to remain in a critical state.Senior officers, including Kushinagar SP Keshav Kumar and other officials, reached the spot during the night and launched an investigation.Based on a complaint filed by the victim’s brother, Abhishek Yadav, an FIR was registered under relevant sections of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita. Investigation led to the identification of the accused.Police arrested two accused — Chhotelal Kharwar and Anuj Kushwaha, both residents of Kushinagar.Kumar said that Chhotelal confessed to being in a relationship with Kajal.“The victim had recently distanced herself as her marriage had been fixed elsewhere,” added Kumar.Enraged, he conspired with his associate and planned the attack. Investigators revealed that the duo arranged the acid from Bihar’s Gopalganj area, Kumar added.
Article Summary: Kajal Yadav, 23, and her mother Leelawati Devi, 58, were attacked at their home in Kushinagar. The victim's mother is battling for life at Gorakhpur Medical College. Police arrested two accused — Chhotelal Kharwar and Anuj Kushwaha.
